old_claim.note: Merchant Courier Networks provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped reports were letters moved between merchant houses and fairs by dedicated fair couriers, making the courier network the delivery mechanism for timely market information.

source_shape.source_locator: Champagne-fairs discussion, pp. 8-10: fair couriers rapidly spread prices of specie and bills, informed merchants of current market conditions, and carried a documented Siena-to-Champagne letter by 1260.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Dedicated fair couriers were the mechanism that transported timely price and market information between Italian merchant houses and Champagne.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The node is explicitly scoped to circulated reports rather than observations made locally at a fair, so transport through the merchant courier network is constitutive of the claim.

ontology_before: Merchant couriers were modeled as one optional enabling route for a broad and weakly defined bulletin concept.
ontology_after: The narrowed node represents courier-carried market reports, making the courier network a required delivery mechanism.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-merchant-market-reports-scope: merchant_courier_networks is required.
would_reject_if: The node is broadened again to include purely local posted notices that were not circulated between markets.
short_reason: The scoped technology is the circulation of market reports, and the documented circulation mechanism was the fair-courier network.
